title: PFAS-free grease-barrier papers move from niche option to mainstream food packaging requirement
summary: Food and pet-food packaging buyers are paying closer attention to how PFAS-free grease-barrier papers perform at folded edges, cut edges and converted formats. The practical message for converters is clear: barrier paper selection must be matched with packaging design, cutting and forming details.
buyerTakeaway: Ask suppliers to confirm PFAS-free test reports, edge performance and whether the paper is suitable for the exact format, such as burger wraps, liners, baking cups or formed trays.
imageAlt: UPM PFAS-free grease barrier paper packaging news image

title: EU PFAS limits for food-contact packaging make August 2026 a key compliance date
summary: The EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation is pushing food-contact packaging buyers to review PFAS limits, packaging components and supplier documentation before the 12 August 2026 application date.
buyerTakeaway: For paper food packaging, buyers should review paper, coating, ink and adhesive documentation together rather than checking the base paper alone.
imageAlt: Food packaging image from Food Safety Magazine PFAS article

title: EUDR keeps traceability high on the agenda for paper and pulp packaging buyers
summary: The EU Deforestation Regulation requires covered products placed on or exported from the EU to be deforestation-free and traceable. For paper packaging buyers, this makes paper sourcing data and due-diligence support increasingly important.
buyerTakeaway: When sourcing paper packaging for Europe, confirm whether the paper is covered by EUDR-related data systems and whether supplier documentation can support importer due diligence.
imageAlt: European Commission EUDR implementation source image

title: Paper, pulp and printing companies prepare for heavier EUDR data collection
summary: EUDR preparation is pushing companies in paper, pulp and printing to collect supplier and raw-material origin data more systematically. Manual document handling is becoming harder as buyers expect clearer traceability.
buyerTakeaway: Choose suppliers that can organize paper sourcing information, certificate files and product documentation in a way that buyers can actually review.
imageAlt: EUDR paper pulp and printing industry news image from osapiens

title: Pulp and paper packaging faces EUDR due-diligence pressure through 2026 and 2027
summary: EUDR guidance for pulp and paper highlights traceability, geolocation data, risk assessment and due-diligence statements. Large and medium enterprises face earlier pressure than small and micro companies.
buyerTakeaway: For European food packaging projects, importers should ask for EUDR-related support early, before confirming mass production or retail launch schedules.
imageAlt: Pulp and paper industry EUDR compliance image from TraceX

title: Baking and cooking paper market forecast points to long-term growth
summary: Market forecasts indicate continued growth for baking and cooking paper, supported by bakery applications, home baking, foodservice and convenience cooking formats.
buyerTakeaway: Baking paper, cupcake liners and release papers are not short-term seasonal products only; they are part of a broader food preparation and bakery packaging growth category.
imageAlt: Baking and cooking paper market image from Future Market Insights

title: Air fryer paper liners remain a high-velocity consumable category
summary: Forecasts for air fryer paper liners point to sustained demand tied to appliance adoption, convenience cooking and reduced cleaning needs in home and commercial kitchens.
buyerTakeaway: Retail and ecommerce buyers should confirm shape, size, perforation, heat resistance, warning labels and packaging format before placing bulk orders.
imageAlt: Air fryer paper liners market image from IndexBox

title: Food contact paper market growth reinforces demand for verified paper packaging suppliers
summary: Market analysis shows food contact paper remains a large and growing packaging category, with demand supported by takeaway, bakery, quick-service restaurant, retail food and sustainability trends.
buyerTakeaway: Buyers should look beyond low unit price and verify food-contact documentation, odor control, printing quality and stable carton delivery.
imageAlt: Food contact paper market image from Fortune Business Insights

title: EU food-contact material discussion expands beyond plastics to inks, coatings and adhesives
summary: Food-contact material policy discussions increasingly cover the full packaging system, including coatings, printing inks, adhesives and other non-plastic components used with food packaging.
buyerTakeaway: For printed greaseproof paper, baking cups and bags, buyers should review ink, coating and adhesive declarations along with the paper itself.

title: PPWR shifts packaging buyer focus toward design, sustainability and documentation
summary: The EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation brings new requirements that affect packaging design, sustainability claims and producer obligations. Food packaging buyers are expected to ask suppliers for clearer technical and compliance support.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery and foodservice paper packaging, prepare product specifications, packaging structure, material declarations and test reports before buyer approval.
imageAlt: EU packaging regulation article image from Gleiss Lutz
